entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum americanum [L.] Leeke, the crop studied for water stress recovery

entities.name: WATER POTENTIAL
entities.description: A physiological measure of water status in leaves

entities.name: ABSCISIC ACID
entities.description: A plant hormone (ABA) whose levels change in response to water stress and rehydration

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: The state of drought stress experienced by the plants

entities.name: REHYDRATION
entities.description: The process of recovering water potential after drought stress

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of water to field-grown plants to initiate rehydration

entities.name: DIURNAL NIGHT-TIME REDUCTION IN EVAPORATIVE DEMAND
entities.description: A natural occurrence that initiates overnight rehydration

entities.name: LIGHT
entities.description: Environmental factor tested for its effect on ABA loss during rehydration

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental factor tested for its effect on the onset of rehydration and ABA loss

entities.name: DARKNESS
entities.description: The absence of light, which was found to accelerate ABA loss during rehydration

relationships.description: Water potential was measured in the leaves of pearl millet
Abscisic acid content was measured in the leaves of pearl millet
Pearl millet plants were subjected to drought stress
Rehydration is the recovery process following water stress

relationships.source: DIURNAL NIGHT-TIME REDUCTION IN EVAPORATIVE DEMAND
relationships.description: Night-time reduction in evaporative demand initiates overnight rehydration
Irrigation results in a reduction of ABA content
The loss of ABA is associated with the process of rehydration
Light prevents or slows the loss of ABA during rehydration
Temperature affects the onset and rate of ABA loss
ABA levels fall more quickly in darkness than in light
